response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with C++ appearing only in a single 6-month role at National Instruments over a decade ago (writing components for a C IDE) and a bachelor thesis project. There is no Windows-platform C++ work, no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no exposure to Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or any capital markets domain — the financial experience at RIDE Capital was pure TypeScript/GraphQL fintech, not capital markets systems. The candidate simply does not satisfy the mandatory requirements of the role.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps practices (GitLab, Docker, CI servers) align with the optional pipeline knowledge requirement
Java exposure from robotic-arm and mobile orchestration work at Altom partially satisfies the optional Java desirable skill
Strong communication evidence: public talks, workshops, and open-source project leadership indicate solid written and verbal ability
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent at a professional level — only ~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, with no subsequent use on any project
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge; the RIDE Capital stint was TypeScript fintech, not trading or treasury systems
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL is listed as a skill but all database work is MongoDB or MySQL, with no enterprise RDBMS depth

key_factors.factor: Capital markets / fixed income domain absence
